title: Veyra DNS TXT Record Specification v1
description: The standard DNS TXT record for AI agent commit mode discovery. Any domain can add a _veyra TXT record to signal commit mode requirements at the DNS layer — before any HTTP connection is made. Analogous to SPF/DKIM/DMARC for email authentication, but for AI write authorization. DNS records are globally cached by resolvers, making this the most scalable discovery mechanism possible.

commit.description: 'required' = all AI writes must use commit mode. 'optional' = commit mode available but not enforced. 'none' = no commit mode needed.

authorize.description: Endpoint to authorize write actions and receive commit tokens.

verify.description: Endpoint for tools to verify commit tokens. Free forever.

class.description: Settlement class. A=low-risk, B=business-critical, C=high-risk, D=cross-org.

discovery_flow: 1. Before any write to {domain}, resolve _veyra.{domain} TXT record
2. If record exists and commit=required, obtain token via authorize endpoint
agent_behavior.caching: DNS TXT records are cached by resolvers according to TTL (recommended: 3600s). Agents SHOULD cache the parsed result for the TTL duration.
agent_behavior.fallback: If DNS lookup fails or no _veyra record exists, fall back to checking /.well-known/veyra.json, then X-Veyra-Commit headers on responses.

why_dns.precedent: SPF (RFC 7208), DKIM (RFC 6376), and DMARC (RFC 7489) used DNS TXT records to make email authentication universal. Over 5 billion DNS lookups per day serve SPF records alone.
why_dns.scale: DNS resolvers cache records globally. A single TXT record addition propagates to every resolver on Earth within hours.
why_dns.zero_http_cost: No HTTP request needed. The agent checks DNS before even opening a connection.
why_dns.universal_infrastructure: Every domain already has DNS. Adding a TXT record takes 30 seconds in any DNS provider.
why_dns.tamper_resistant: DNS records are controlled by the domain owner. They cannot be injected or spoofed by intermediaries (with DNSSEC).

implementation.for_tool_builders: Add a TXT record: _veyra.yourdomain.com → v=veyra1; commit=required; authorize=https://api.veyra.dev/v1/authorize-action; verify=https://api.veyra.dev/v1/verify-token
implementation.for_agent_developers: const record = await dns.resolveTxt('_veyra.' + domain); const policy = parseVeyraRecord(record);
implementation.sdk: const policy = await veyra.checkDNS('salesforce.com')  // checks _veyra.salesforce.com TXT
